strengths: Meets the minimum requirement of 15+ years of software engineering experience, including entrepreneurial experience as the founder of GISCollective.
Demonstrated cloud and infrastructure engineering skills, utilizing AWS CDK, Kubernetes, and microservices architectures in roles at Optilyz and Dunnhumby.
Proven ability to inspire and share insights externally, evidenced by creating multiple workshops, giving talks (e.g., FOSS4G 2019), and contributing to open-source.
concerns: Lacks the preferred 'recognized expertise in AI' and a sustained track record of building AI-powered platforms, with AI experience limited to a very recent role at re:cinq.
No evidence of operating at a Director-level capacity or driving high-level technical strategy for large-scale enterprise cloud or AI platforms.
Missing experience in advising strategic customers and partners on cutting-edge AI and cloud adoption, which is a key responsibility for this role.

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI Expertise (absence of long-term AI product development)
Strategic Customer Advising (absence of Director-level consulting)

response.justification: While the candidate meets the 15-year minimum engineering experience requirement and possesses strong entrepreneurial and cloud infrastructure skills (e.g., founding GISCollective and using AWS/Kubernetes), they fall short of the core requirements for a Director-level AI Strategy role. Their resume lacks the preferred recognized expertise in AI, with AI-related work appearing only in their most recent position at re:cinq involving MCP code generators. Furthermore, there is a distinct absence of experience advising strategic enterprise customers on AI adoption or operating at a Director-level strategic capacity, making them a poor fit for this highly specialized, leadership-oriented AI Principal Engineer position.
